title: delphinium decorum var. nevadense
intro: Delphinium decorum var. nevadense, commonly known as Nevada larkspur, is a perennial plant native to the western United States and Canada. As a member of the Delphinium genus, it is known for its tall, stately spikes of blue or purple flowers and its medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used for its antispasmodic and antitussive properties, helping to relieve muscle spasms and coughs. In herbal preparations, Nevada larkspur is often made into a tea or tincture, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from bronchitis to insomnia, and is also valued for its ability to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation.
